section.title: Ethics and governance
section.intro: AI ethics frameworks, governance gaps, and how far published principles are from actual implementation.

resources.title: AI in Public Health: Promises, Challenges, and a Research Agenda
resources.description: European Observatory and WHO agenda. Documents the gap between AI capability and the public health infrastructure needed to use it.

resources.title: Informed Consent, Redefined
resources.description: Traditional informed consent assumes specific, bounded data uses. AI processing is neither. Legal and ethical frameworks haven't caught up.

resources.title: Environmental Impact of AI Servers
resources.description: Estimates 731–1,125 million cubic meters of water and 24–44 megatons of CO2 annually from AI infrastructure. Each large data center consumes as much energy as 100,000 households.

resources.title: Patient Consent for Secondary Use of Data in AI: A Scoping Review
resources.description: Scoping review on how consent operates when data enters AI training sets. Once incorporated, data shapes all future predictions, making consent effectively irrevocable.

resources.title: Governing AI in the World of Work
resources.description: Ethics guidelines for AI in employment across jurisdictions. Documents where principle and practice diverge.

resources.title: HealthAI: The Global Agency for Responsible AI in Health
resources.description: Geneva-based nonprofit with 200+ institutional members coordinating a Global Regulatory Network for health AI. Rebranded from I-DAIR Foundation.

resources.title: AI Consent Guide for IRB Protocols
resources.description: Consent language templates for research protocols involving AI.

resources.title: SACHRP Terminated – What It Means for Research Oversight
resources.description: SACHRP was terminated in March 2025 as part of HHS restructuring. Its September 2024 recommendations on AI and human subjects research remain relevant, but the committee that would update federal guidance no longer exists.

resources.title: Equity-First Standards for Health AI
resources.description: 75-page report calling for bias audits, transparency reports, and community governance councils for health AI.

resources.title: AI in Early Warning Systems for Infectious Disease Surveillance: A Systematic Review
resources.description: 67 studies on AI in outbreak early warning systems. ML, NLP, and deep learning approaches. Persistent challenges: data quality, model transparency, privacy, and equity.

resources.title: Ethical Issues Raised by AI and Big Data in Population Health
resources.description: Scoping review of ethical issues in AI-driven population health: consent, labor, data sovereignty, and evidence of health data colonialism where AI researchers in high-income countries acquire LMIC data to avoid stricter regulations.

resources.title: GPT-4 Predicts Treatment Effects Across 70 Experiments
resources.author: Hewitt et al.
resources.description: r=0.85 correlation between GPT-4 predictions and actual treatment effects across 70 preregistered experiments. Gelman et al. argue the validation methods are inadequate.

resources.title: This Human Study Did Not Involve Human Subjects
resources.author: Hullman et al.
resources.description: Argues that studies using AI-generated synthetic participants sidestep human subjects review through a definitional loophole, not through genuine ethical analysis.
